paper_title: Propolis Sample Analysis in Northeastern Brazil

methods.description: Collection of propolis samples from various locations in northeastern Brazil to ensure diverse phytophysiognomies are represented.

steps.description: The samples were obtained from several states in northeastern Brazil, including Alagoas, Bahia, Ceará, Maranhão, Paraíba, Pernambuco, Piauí, and Sergipe.
steps.searchable_summary: collect propolis samples from specified locations in northeastern Brazil

steps.description: The samples were collected by shaving the internal walls of boxes, borders, nests, and roofs of beehives, and then stored hermetically in tagged containers.
steps.searchable_summary: shave and store propolis samples in tagged containers

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing pollen content in propolis samples to determine the frequency distribution of pollen types.

steps.description: A subsample of 0.5 grams of propolis was separated for dilution for at least 24 hours in 10 ml of absolute ethanol.
The preparations were then centrifuged (2500 rpm for 10 min) and the resulting pellets boiled in 10% KOH for 5 minutes in a moist chamber.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge and boil pellets in KOH

steps.description: The material was then filtered twice by discarding the water and placed in 5 ml of glacial acetic acid where it was kept for 2 to 12 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: filter and treat pellets with glacial acetic acid

steps.description: The pellets were subsequently submitted to acetolysis in a moist chamber at up to 80 ºC for nearly 2 min.
The preparations were then washed in distilled water, centrifuged and stored for 1 to 24 hours in a 50% glycerin aqueous solution.
steps.searchable_summary: wash, centrifuge, and store pellets in glycerin solution

steps.description: The pollen sediment was mounted on slides (five per sample), with glycerin jelly, for microscopic analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: mount pollen sediment on slides for microscopy

methods.description: Determination of the frequency distribution of pollen types in the collected samples.

steps.description: The relative frequency of pollen types in each sample was based on counts of at least 500 grains of pollen per sample.
steps.searchable_summary: count pollen grains to determine frequency distribution

methods.description: Analysis of floristic similarity among samples using a dendrogram and Jaccard's similarity coefficient.

steps.description: Analysis of floristic similarity among samples was performed by constructing a dendrogram using the software PAST-Palaeontological Statistics, ver.1.89 with Jaccard's similarity coefficient.
steps.searchable_summary: construct dendrogram using PAST software and Jaccard's coefficient
